article_title: FDA Approves Decnupaz, the First CD123-Targeting ADC for a Blood Cancer With Historically Dismal Prognosis
summary: Submission approvable with 2 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: FDA Approves Decnupaz, the First CD123-Targeting ADC for a Blood Cancer With Historically Dismal Prognosis
content_preview.summary: AbbVie's pivekimab sunirine-pvzy wins FDA approval for BPDCN on May 27, with a 69.7% complete remission rate in newly diagnosed patients and the first-ever outpatient initiation for this ultra-rare malignancy.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The U.S. Food and Drug Administration on May 27, 2026 approved pivekimab sunirine-pvzy (Decnupaz), a CD123-directed antibody-drug conjugate developed by AbbVie, for adults with blastic plasmacytoid dendritic cell neoplasm (BPDCN), an ultra-rare and aggressive blood cancer for which treatment options have been sharply limited, [according to AbbVie's press release on PR Newswire](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/us-fda-approves-decnupaztm-pivekimab-sunirine-pvzy-for-treatment-...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt
1 source(s) blocked bot access — sources likely valid but not archivable automatically

editor_notes.content_quality: Comprehensive biotech/medicine news article on FDA approval of Decnupaz for BPDCN. Well-structured with disease background, trial data, safety, regulatory history, and investigator quotes. 825 words fits News category.
editor_notes.source_verification: source-0.html.gz (PR Newswire): confirmed approval date May 27 2026, 69.7% CR/CRc rate, 39.4% SCT rate, 9.7 months DOR, October 2020 BTD, both investigator quotes verbatim, boxed warning, adverse events. source-1.html.gz (AbbVie news): confirmed both quotes verbatim, trial data. source-2.html.gz (ONS): confirmed 0.045 mg/kg dosing every 3 weeks over 15-30 minutes, 51 R/R patients, 15.7% CR/CRc, 9.2 months DOR, 24.1 months follow-up, 11.8% SCT rate in R/R. source-3.html.gz (StockTitan): confirmed trial data and quotes. source-4 (AllSci): HTTP 403 - cannot verify AllSci-attributed claims: (1) mechanism description 'indolinobenzodiazepine pseudodimer payload that alkylates DNA, causing single-strand breaks without crosslinking' and (2) ImmunoGen acquisition for $10.1 billion in November 2023. Note: PR Newswire (source-0) confirms the DNA single-strand break mechanism, partially corroborating claim 1; the ImmunoGen acquisition price is widely documented public record. source-5.html.gz (Elzonris): confirmed 8-14 months median OS, 60s/70s age, skin lesions in ~90%. source-6.html.gz (NCBI StatPearls): confirmed tagraxofusp 2018 FDA approval, BPDCN disease characteristics. Note: StatPearls describes tagraxofusp as 'recombinant human interleukin-3 fused to a truncated diphtheria toxin' - the article's claim it 'works by inhibiting protein synthesis' is not explicitly stated in this snapshot but is consistent with diphtheria toxin's known mechanism.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Core clinical data all verified. Two AllSci-attributed claims unverifiable due to 403 block: (1) the specific chemical name 'indolinobenzodiazepine pseudodimer' (mechanism substance is corroborated by PR Newswire); (2) '$10.1 billion' and 'November 2023' for ImmunoGen acquisition (widely known but not in available snapshots). These are subordinate body claims, not in headline or lead. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS is appropriate.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional. No sensationalism. Appropriately balanced with safety information.
editor_notes.originality: Novel topic - no prior Decnupaz/BPDCN articles found in archive.
concerns: AllSci source returned HTTP 403 - two specific claims attributed to it cannot be independently verified from snapshots: the indolinobenzodiazepine pseudodimer mechanism name and the $10.1 billion ImmunoGen acquisition figure.
recommendations: File corrections noting AllSci source was inaccessible and specific claims cannot be snapshot-verified.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality FDA approval coverage with strong trial data. Two unverifiable AllSci-attributed specifics warrant a corrections note. Overall factually sound and well-sourced.
